lines.english: Blessed is that grain, by which the heart-lotus of the hungry blossoms forth.
Blessed is the Lord of the World. Blessed is the Divine Guru.
Gond:
This grain comes from the Primal Lord God.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਨੁ ਓਇ ਸੰਤ ਜਿਨ ਐਸੀ ਜਾਨੀ ॥
lines.translit: dhan oe sant jin aisee jaanee |
lines.english: Blessed are those Saints, who know this.
Meeting with them, one meets the Lord, the Sustainer of the World. ||1||
Mixed with water, its taste becomes sublime.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਪੀਐ ਨਾਮੁ ਅੰਨ ਕੈ ਸਾਦਿ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: japeeai naam an kai saad |1| rahaau |
lines.english: One chants the Naam, the Name of the Lord, only when he tastes this grain. ||1||Pause||
Meditate on the Naam, and meditate on this grain.
loses his honor in the three worlds. ||2||
She is neither a happy soul-bride, nor a widow.
One who discards this grain, is practicing hypocrisy.
One who abstains from this grain,
Those who claim in this world that they live on milk alone,
secretly eat whole loads of food. ||3||
Without this grain, time does not pass in peace.
Forsaking this grain, one does not meet the Lord of the World.
Says Kabeer, this I know:
blessed is that grain, which brings faith in the Lord and Master to the mind. ||4||8||11||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਗੁ ਗੋਂਡ ਬਾਣੀ ਨਾਮਦੇਉ ਜੀ ਕੀ ਘਰੁ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: raag gondd baanee naamadeo jee kee ghar 1 |
lines.english: Raag Gond, The Word Of Naam Dayv Jee, First House:
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:
The ritual sacrifice of horses,
and ceremonial cleansing baths -||1||
giving one's weight in gold to charities,

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਉ ਨ ਪੁਜਹਿ ਹਰਿ ਕੀਰਤਿ ਨਾਮਾ ॥
lines.translit: tau na pujeh har keerat naamaa |
lines.english: These are not equal to singing the Praises of the Lord's Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਪੁਨੇ ਰਾਮਹਿ ਭਜੁ ਰੇ ਮਨ ਆਲਸੀਆ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: apune raameh bhaj re man aalaseea |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Meditate on your Lord, you lazy man! ||1||Pause||
Offering sweet rice at Gaya,
living on the river banks at Benares,
reciting the four Vedas by heart;||2||
restraining sexual passion by the spiritual wisdom given by the Guru,
Completing all religious rituals,
and performing the six rituals;||3||
O man, renounce and abandon all these things.
Expounding on Shiva and Shakti
Meditate, meditate in remembrance on the Lord of the Universe.
Meditate, O Naam Dayv, and cross over the terrifying world-ocean. ||4||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਾਨ ਤਜੇ ਵਾ ਕੋ ਧਿਆਨੁ ਨ ਜਾਏ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: praan taje vaa ko dhiaan na jaae |1|
lines.english: it loses its life, but it cannot stop thinking about it. ||1||
The deer is lured by the sound of the hunter's bell;
In the same way, I look upon my Lord.
Gond:
As the fisherman looks upon the fish,

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮੁ ਛੋਡਿ ਚਿਤੁ ਅਨਤ ਨ ਫੇਰਉ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: raam chhodd chit anat na ferau |1| rahaau |
lines.english: I will not abandon my Lord, and turn my thoughts to another. ||1||Pause||
and the goldsmith looks upon the gold he fashions;||2||
As the man driven by sex looks upon another man's wife,

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਹ ਜਹ ਦੇਖਉ ਤਹ ਤਹ ਰਾਮਾ ॥
lines.translit: jeh jeh dekhau teh teh raamaa |
lines.english: In the same way, wherever Naam Dayv looks, he sees the Lord.
and the gambler looks upon the throwing of the dice -||3||
Gond:

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੋ ਕਉ ਤਾਰਿ ਲੇ ਰਾਮਾ ਤਾਰਿ ਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: mo kau taar le raamaa taar le |
lines.english: Carry me across, O Lord, carry me across.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕੇ ਚਰਨ ਨਿਤ ਧਿਆਵੈ ਨਾਮਾ ॥੪॥੨॥
lines.translit: har ke charan nit dhiaavai naamaa |4|2|
lines.english: Naam Dayv meditates continuously on the Feet of the Lord. ||4||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਰ ਤੇ ਸੁਰ ਹੋਇ ਜਾਤ ਨਿਮਖ ਮੈ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਬੁਧਿ ਸਿਖਲਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: nar te sur hoe jaat nimakh mai satigur budh sikhalaaee |
lines.english: I have been transformed from a mortal being into an angel, in an instant; the True Guru has taught me this.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੈ ਅਜਾਨੁ ਜਨੁ ਤਰਿਬੇ ਨ ਜਾਨਉ ਬਾਪ ਬੀਠੁਲਾ ਬਾਹ ਦੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: mai ajaan jan taribe na jaanau baap beetthulaa baah de |1| rahaau |
lines.english: I am ignorant, and I do not know how to swim. O my Beloved Father, please give me Your arm. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਰ ਤੇ ਉਪਜਿ ਸੁਰਗ ਕਉ ਜੀਤਿਓ ਸੋ ਅਵਖਧ ਮੈ ਪਾਈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: nar te upaj surag kau jeetio so avakhadh mai paaee |1|
lines.english: Born of human flesh, I have conquered the heavens; such is the medicine I was given. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੇਰੇ ਨਾਮ ਅਵਿਲੰਬਿ ਬਹੁਤੁ ਜਨ ਉਧਰੇ ਨਾਮੇ ਕੀ ਨਿਜ ਮਤਿ ਏਹ ॥੨॥੩॥
lines.translit: tere naam avilanb bahut jan udhare naame kee nij mat eh |2|3|
lines.english: With the Support of Your Name, so many have been saved; this is Naam Dayv's understanding. ||2||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਹਾ ਜਹਾ ਧੂਅ ਨਾਰਦੁ ਟੇਕੇ ਨੈਕੁ ਟਿਕਾਵਹੁ ਮੋਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: jahaa jahaa dhooa naarad tteke naik ttikaavahu mohi |
lines.english: Please place me where You placed Dhroo and Naarad, O my Master.
